title: Post-call recap + bench ask: hyperscaler gap-proof benchmarking
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Park <emily.park@skylinehealth.com>, Jason Liu <jason.liu@hypercloud.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-11-07T15:20:00-08:00
Subject: Post-call recap + bench ask: hyperscaler gap-proof benchmarking

Thanks Emily/Jason — good call today. Quick written recap and the minimal ask so we can produce an apples-to-apples bench readout next week.

Summary (what we covered)
- Skyline is evaluating end-user latency and cost tradeoffs between running inference on Hyperscaler-managed endpoints and Redwood Dedicated.
- Priority signals: p95/p99 tail latency for 1–8 concurrent users, steady-state tokens/sec, and cold/warm start behavior for short prompts (~40–120 tokens).
- You want a short, reproducible bench run we can share with graphs and a CSV of raw samples to present to your infrastructure committee.

What Redwood will deliver
- A/B run plan that compares: Hyperscaler endpoint (your managed instance) vs Redwood Dedicated pool (configured with identical model variant, request payloads, and concurrency profile).
- Measurements: p50/p95/p99 latency, tokens/sec (sustained at target QPS), throughput curves for concurrency sweep, and memory/KV cache hit notes.
- Both cold-start and warm-run captures (we'll seed KV cache for warm runs).
- Raw results CSV + an analyst-friendly summary (PNG charts + short one-page takeaway).

What we need from you to start
1) Representative prompt set (10–20 prompts) — can be synthetic but must reflect your user mix (transactional vs long-form). PDF or plain CSV is fine.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Post-call recap + bench ask: hyperscaler gap-proof benchmarking

Emily — thanks. Quick, concrete ranges and the exact A/B approach we recommend. Also looping in Monica / Logan who will run the tests.

Expected guardrail ranges (based on recent similar runs):
- Small payload (~40 tokens): expected p95 gap between Hyperscaler-managed endpoint and Redwood Dedicated: 40–180 ms (Redwood lower in most configs when prefetched/KV caching enabled). p99 gap: 80–300 ms.
- Medium payload (~120 tokens): expected p95 gap: 80–300 ms. p99 gap: 150–550 ms.
- Tokens/sec (sustained): on a 1-GPU equivalent serving pool, steady-state throughput for this model class typically lands between ~1,000–2,200 tokens/sec depending on batching strategy and quantization; Redwood's continuous-batching config aims to trade a small p50 increase for higher tokens/sec.

A/B methodology (client-driven single-runner approach)
- Single runner alternates traffic buckets to Hyperscaler endpoint and Redwood endpoint in 60s windows so both endpoints see identical prompt sequence and timing. This keeps load visible to your SREs and avoids cross-account interference.
- For warm runs we'll pre-warm with 100 requests to seed KV / caches; cold runs will start from an idle runner after a 30+ minute quiet period.
- Concurrency sweep: 1, 4, 8, 16. Each bucket executes 500 requests per endpoint per payload size (small/medium).
- Measurement export: per-request latency, server-side trace id (if available), tokens generated, and a column for vCPU/GPU instance type used on the Hyperscaler side.

Engineering ask (who does what)
- Monica: stand up Redwood runner + perf instrumentation; export CSV and charts.
- Logan: coordinate with your SRE (Emily) on the client-runner and share the test harness (we'll provide a lightweight open-source runner).

We will attach a concrete bench matrix (attached gapproof-bench-plan.csv) and populate the results-template.xlsx during the run.

If this looks good, Emily — please send the prompt CSV and the Hyperscaler endpoint contact and we’ll lock a Tue morning slot.

Best — Ben

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>

Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick recap + next artifacts from today’s discovery (models, perf links)

Hi Claire / Carlos — thanks again for the time today. Short recap and the items we promised to share so your engineering and clinical AI teams can triage model fit: 

1) Goal recap — clarify success metrics: throughput goal ~1.5k concurrent chats (peak), P95 latency target <= 450ms, and no regression on chart-extraction F1 (current baseline you mentioned ~0.87).

2) Suggested starting model family — Redwood suggests evaluating two variants: an accuracy-first 13B quantized fp16 path (recommended for clinical extraction quality) and a faster 7B int8 variant for higher TPS. Both are in the attached "redwood_tps-profile.pdf" with Redwood-verified TPS (tokens/sec) and latency percentiles measured on an equivalent GPU profile.

3) Benchmarks and prompt sets — sharing a small prompt-pack (clarity_prompt-pack-v1.jsonl) and a snapshot CSV of our synthetic throughput/latency sweep (benchmark-kpi-snapshot.csv). Links below and attached copies in this thread. These are the same artifacts we use internally to align ops decisions.

4) Quality/regression note — when switching quantization profiles we often see subtle extraction regressions (entity truncation on long notes). Recommended step: run the prompt-pack evaluation and compare the F1 on your ground truth subset before promoting a faster profile. We can help automate this as part of the pilot.

Next steps (suggested):
- You run the prompt-pack against the two model variants (we can provide a small runner).
- We provide a one-hour profiling session with Ben to walk the TPS/latency curves and safe rollout options (canary and fallback to the higher-accuracy model).
- Tentative pilot start: Nov 15 if that aligns with your infra schedule.

Drive + S3 links: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/clarityhealth/benchmarks/2027-11-02 and https://s3.redwood-inference.com/benchmarks/cl-2027/clarity_tps_snapshot.csv

Let me know if you want us to kick off the profiling session this week.

Best,
Avery
Avery Johnson

- benchmark-kpi-snapshot.csv (raw sweep results)

Claire — quick follow-up with the measured numbers you asked about. Pulled the rows from the sweep that match your target workload (batching ~8, sequence len 256 typical notes). Summary inlined and raw CSV attached. Full results + runner are on the Drive link.

- Latency P95: 410ms (P50: 180ms, P90: 360ms)

The primary risk is rare degradation on long clinical notes — we saw entity truncation on ~0.8% of synthetic long-form cases using the faster profile (we tracked that in the prompt-pack eval). For the 2k de-identified sample Claire mentioned we recommend running both profiles and comparing extraction F1 before switching live.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@clarityhealth.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Nov 2027 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Quick recap + next artifacts from today’s discovery (models, perf links)

Claire — attached is a short pre-call checklist and a recommended canary playbook snippet I’ll walk through on Nov 10. Key monitoring points (short):
- GPU util: target < 78% steady-state to allow headroom for spikes.
- NVMe: sustained writes for KV cache < 150 MB/s; watch for stalls > 200ms.
- Request queue depth: keep average < 12 per worker process; tail > 25 indicates need to scale.

Canary thresholds we use: start at 5% traffic, monitor extraction F1 delta (stop if drop > 0.02 absolute), P95 latency increase < 10% over baseline, and error rate < 0.2%. If any trigger trips, auto-fallback to 13B accuracy path and scale up dedicated workers.

Also uploaded a small runner and the checklist to the Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/clarityhealth/benchmarks/2027-11-02

See you Wed Nov 10 @10:00 PT — Ben, Jordan and I will be on the call.

Ben

- Request queue depth: keep average < 12 per worker process; tail > 25 indicates need to scale.

Canary thresholds we use: start at 5% traffic, monitor extraction F1 delta (stop if drop > 0.02 absolute), P95 latency increase < 10% over baseline, and error rate < 0.2%. If any trigger trips, auto-fallback to 13B accuracy path and scale up dedicated workers.

Also uploaded a small runner and the checklist to the Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/clarityhealth/benchmarks/2027-11-02

See you Wed Nov 10 @10:00 PT — Ben, Jordan and I will be on the call.

Ben

Subject: Re: Demo time showed up wrong after forward (DST anchor mismatch) - SecuroBank

Thanks for the details — very helpful reproduction notes. Short summary and next steps:

  1) What happened: Rachel (Redwood EU rep) forwarded the original invite from a Europe/London calendar just before the US DST rollback (Nov 7). When the forward was processed by some external tenants, the .ics lost a TZID anchor and became a "floating" time. That meant recipients' calendars interpreted the 18:00 value as local time, which for Maria's London tenant after the DST rollback mapped to 23:00 local for her configured timezone.

  2) Immediate fix: I'm attaching a regenerated .ics that explicitly sets DTSTART/DTEND with TZID=Europe/London and includes the VTIMEZONE block for standard time. Please delete the current event and accept the new invite. Attachments: securobank-demo-join.ics (updated) + calendar-screenshot.png (what Maria saw).

  3) Prevention: Marissa/Platform — can we add a quick checklist for EU reps when forwarding invites across tenants around DST boundaries? Simple rule: resend anchored .ics (include VTIMEZONE) instead of forwarding. Also Karthik, please send the meeting prep deck to Maria directly rather than rely on the calendar payload.

I'll send the updated invite in 10 minutes. Apologies for the churn — want to keep this simple for SecuroBank's schedule.

Cheers,
Ben Carter

Attachments: securobank-demo-join.ics (updated), calendar-screenshot.png

title: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Liam - thanks for the call earlier. Per Acme's private VPC deployment requirements, we want to nail down where TLS should terminate and how DNS for the private endpoints will be delegated. Attached is a rough architecture diagram (tls-architecture-v2.pdf) and an example CSR template (csr-example.txt).

Key questions to confirm so we can finalize the onboarding checklist:
- Termination point: do you require TLS to be terminated on your LB (customer-edge) for compliance, or can we accept TLS passthrough to Redwood's private bridge where we present (or accept) client certs?
- Client auth: if you prefer passthrough, can your LB forward raw TCP to Redwood, preserving SNI? If not, we need an alternate plan for mutual client TLS.
- DNS delegation: would you be comfortable delegating a subdomain (e.g., redwood-acme.internal.acmehealth) to your VPC resolver or to a delegated zone we manage? Any preference for CNAME vs split-horizon approach?
- Certificate issuance: do you want Redwood to manage certs via ACME (automated) or will you provide signed certs from your PKI? If the latter, we need the signing CA chain and preferred SAN policy (wildcards vs explicit SANs).
- Logging & audit: any specific requirements for TLS session logs, OCSP stapling, or certificate rotation windows?

Proposed next step: short technical sync (30m) this week with your networking and security leads. If you can reply with the preferred termination option and PKI stance, we’ll prepare the CSR mapping and a minimal config diff for your LB.

Thanks,
Ben
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: tls-architecture-v2.pdf; csr-example.txt)

From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: devops@acmehealth.com
Date: Tue, 2 Nov 2027 13:45:00 -0000
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Ben — appreciate the diagram. Short answers from our side: we have a strict policy that TLS must terminate at the Acme edge for PHI-scoped traffic; that said, for lower-sensitivity endpoints we can consider passthrough to Redwood if the TCP path preserves SNI and client IP.

Our PKI: we operate an internal CA (vault-backed) and generally prefer signing CSRs ourselves. We can provide an intermediate CA chain and support short-lived certs (90 days max). OCSP is required for external-facing certs; for private endpoints we can accept OCSP stapling from Redwood, but we need a copy of the OCSP responder URL and proof of stapling in the first 3 rotations.

DNS: we can delegate a narrow subdomain to our resolver (delegate.redwood.acme.internal) or conversely accept a CNAME into our internal zone. Split-horizon is preferred so external records are not leaked.

We'll need an explicit list of FQDNs you plan to present (by env: staging/prod) so our security team can pre-authorize SANs.

Liam
Head of Network Security, Acme Health
(Attachment: none)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Quoting Liam's note above — a few technical follow-ups from infra: 

1) If Acme terminates TLS at their LB, we need a secure, authenticated backend channel (mTLS) between their LB and our private runtime. Can their LB present client certs to the Redwood bridge, or do we need to configure an IP allowlist + mutual-TLS?

2) SNI mapping: to preserve tenant routing we prefer a per-tenant FQDN (tenantX.redwood-acme.internal). Wildcards make routing harder for metrics and isolation; do they have a policy against issuing wildcards? If they can only sign wildcards from their CA, we'll need explicit SAN lists per tenant.

3) OCSP & stapling: we can staple OCSP from our side if Redwood holds the leaf certs, but if Acme controls issuance we need a test vector to validate stapling on production TLS handshakes. Please confirm their OCSP responder URL and whether they support stapling for internally-signed certs.

4) Rotation windows: propose 30-day staggered rotations for our presented certs so there's no mass expiry across tenant endpoints. Will their CA support automated renewal via an API, or will renewals be a manual CSR process?

I'll pull together a small matrix (mutual-tls-mapping.csv) that maps termination option -> required artifacts.

—Jordan
Infra Lead, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: mutual-tls-mapping.csv)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Nov 2027 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Adding product/ops perspective: 

- Operational impact: if Acme insists on edge termination, our observability changes — we will not see token-level latency post-termination and will rely on their LB logs for request tracing. We'll need inbound logging hooks (structured) from Acme to map to Redwood request ids.

- SLA/Failover: confirm whether Acme will accept us failing back to a regional hosted fallback (non-private) in the event of VPC connectivity loss. This affects our continuity plan and whether we include a short TTL (60s) on delegated records.

- Compliance: Karthik, please confirm whether an internal intermediate CA (provided by Acme) requires HSM-backed key storage for us to accept their signed certs under our policy. If so, we'll need their crypto attestation.

Marissa
Director, Product Operations, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Nov 2027 16:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: TLS: edge vs client-bridge — DNS delegation & cert ops for private deployment

Thanks everyone — summary and action items: 

1) Acme prefers edge termination for PHI-scoped endpoints; for non-PHI we may use TCP passthrough if SNI preserved. Liam to confirm list of PHI-scoped FQDNs by EOD Friday. (Owner: Liam)

2) PKI approach: Acme will sign CSRs using their internal CA and provide an intermediate chain. They will support 90-day certs; we request they expose an API or a documented manual renewal flow. (Owner: Acme + Karthik for policy confirmation)

3) DNS: Acme to delegate delegate.redwood.acme.internal via split-horizon. Redwood to provide initial FQDN list for staging/prod. (Owner: Ben)

4) OCSP and stapling: Acme to provide OCSP responder URL and test vector; Redwood will validate stapling in staging before prod cutover. (Owner: Jordan + Liam)

5) Ops & logging: Acme to share LB structured logs schema and sampling for request-id correlation. Redwood will prepare a minimal logging adapter. (Owner: Marissa + Acme DevOps)

Next step: schedule 30-minute technical sync Thursday 11/6 at 14:00 PT to walk through the CSR fields and DNS delegation steps. I've created a Jira ticket and uploaded the diagram/CSR template to the Drive folder (links below).

Links & artifacts:
- Diagram & CSR: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cACME-Private-Deploy
- Jira ticket: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RI-ACME-8243

Attachments: tls-architecture-v2.pdf; csr-example.txt; mutual-tls-mapping.csv

Ben
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

title: Vulnerability management evidence submission and SBOM inventory — NorthCoast Bank
preview:
From: Dana Cole <security@northcoastbank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procure@northcoastbank.com
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2027 09:14:00 -0500

  * SAST: SynScan Enterprise (internal orchestration) + semgrep open-source rules. Gate: every PR, critical findings block merge; weekly aggregated report.

  * Third-party dependency scanning: Dependabot + nightly SBOM reconciliation job.

- Patch SLAs (see patch_sla_matrix.xlsx attached):

  * High (7–8.9): 7 days to remediation with backstop escalations to infra on-call.
  * Medium/Low: 30/90 days respectively, tracked via scheduled sprints.
  * Enforcement: Jira tickets auto-created from scanner alerts; overdue tickets trigger page to on-call and exec alert if > SLA.

- Compensating controls: temporary routing to a hardened pool, WAF rule exceptions, feature flag disablement, and traffic segmentation. All mitigations are documented in the ticket with planned remediation ETA.

- SBOM: attached CSV is a flattened export for the current hosted runtime image (includes package name, version, purl). We can also produce SPDX if they prefer.

Emily / Rajesh —

High-level objective: Go-live for inference integration in PRD by 2028-01-15 with staged traffic ramp and SOC2 evidence in place for procurement sign-off.

Sprint‑Zero (week of 2027-11-13) — goals and owners:
- Access & network baseline (due 2027-11-15) — Owner: Lauren Bishop (Redwood).
- Security questionnaire + evidence list (due 2027-11-17) — Owner: Soojin Lee (Redwood), deliverable: compliance-evidence-list.xlsx.
- Initial model selection & inference cadence (due 2027-11-20) — Owner: Ben Carter (Redwood), input: Springboard usage profile.
- Dev/test workspace ready (due 2027-11-22) — Owner: Springboard Eng (Rajesh).

Milestone map (high level):
1) Sprint‑Zero complete — 2027-11-22 — gate: VPN + test tokens provisioned.
2) Pilot (limited traffic, validation) — 2027-12-15 — gate: performance SLOs met.
3) Go‑live (gradual ramp) — 2028-01-15 — gate: procurement sign-off + DocuSign executed.

Immediate asks from our side:
- Can you confirm the right security contact for itemized evidence? (Emily / Procurement)
- Please confirm Rajesh's lead dev availability the week of 11/13 for a 2-hour kickoff.

Next step: I'll update the MAP with any changes and share a Drive folder. If this looks good I’ll propose a brief kickoff (30–45m) on Monday 11/13 10am PT.

Thanks —
Ben

From: Priya Shah <security@luminarahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aaron Park <aaron.park@luminarahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: CAIQ intake + evidence window request

Hi Ben,

Thanks for helping coordinate — we (Luminara Health) are kicking off our vendor CAIQ and SOC2 intake for the upcoming audit window. Can we schedule a 45-minute technical intake to review the CAIQ you've filled, confirm evidence handoff cadence, and validate encryption key ownership?

High-level asks for that call:
- Walkthrough of your completed CAIQ (we expect the filled worksheet).
- Sample logs for authentication and admin activity (last 90 days preferred).
- Confirmation of KMS/HSM usage and the person/team responsible for key custody.
- Retention/archival policy artifacts and egress controls.

We have availability Wed 11/3 10–12 PST or Thu 11/4 08–11 PST. If those don't work, propose alternatives. I attached a short intake checklist (see attachment: vendor-intake-checklist.pdf).

Priya Shah
Security Operations Lead
Luminara Health
security@luminarahealth.com

--

- KMS/HSM: we use AWS KMS in a customer-isolated CMK configuration for hosted customers; I will route a short owner confirmation before the call.

Marissa will cover dataflow and log locations on the call; Jordan is the owner for key custody and will join to confirm access matrix.

Action items before Wed 10:30 PST:
1) Ben: upload CAIQ_v2_filled.xlsx and log-samples.tar.gz to shared Drive and attach.
2) Marissa: draft short notes on log retention and archival policy.
3) Jordan: confirm KMS usage and prepare encryption-key-access-matrix.pdf.

Hi Aisha,

Hi Elena — thanks for the note and the context.

- Signed W-9 (US entities)

- Bank remit instructions (voided check or bank details PDF)
- Short SOW or invoice template showing line items and total
- Company registration or VAT/Tax ID (if applicable)

Operational guardrails we typically require for temporary no-PO:
- 90-day invoice-only period with a hard spend cap (suggested cap: $15,000 for this pilot)

- Payment terms default to Net30 unless Finance approves otherwise

title: SLO probe spec handoff and reproducibility agreement
Advance collection mechanics + VAT responsibility for Order Form (Acme)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>
Cc: procurement@acme.com
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 09:08:00 +0100
Subject: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Hi Asha,

Thanks again for the productive call this morning. Per our discussion, I attached the current Order Form (Acme_Orderform_v3.pdf). Two items Finance flagged we should lock down before legal redlines:

1) Advance collection (upfront credit) — Acme asked whether the upfront payment can be treated as a prepayment or whether Redwood should invoice and collect in advance and then apply to monthly usage. We usually issue an invoice for the upfront credit and apply it to usage; Finance wants to confirm whether Acme needs any special PO/invoice sequencing.

2) VAT / tax liability — you mentioned that Acme will be responsible for VAT in your jurisdiction, but your procurement team asked whether Redwood can show VAT on our invoice or whether Acme will self-account. If Acme will self-account, we need a tax residency certificate or OSS evidence so we can issue a net invoice.

Can you confirm: (a) preferred invoice flow for the upfront credit (single upfront invoice vs. staged invoices tied to tranches), and (b) whether Acme will provide the tax evidence (attached form noted as Acme_Tax_Cert_2027.pdf) or expects Redwood to tax the invoices?

If you prefer, I can loop in Kimberly from Finance and Karthik from Legal to propose straightforward language for the Order Form to avoid back-and-forth redlines.

Thanks — aiming to get this locked so Legal can finish the MSA/order form review tomorrow.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
attachments: Acme_Orderform_v3.pdf


From: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@acme.com, J. Peterson - Acme Legal <j.peterson@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 10:32:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Ben — thanks, that matches our notes. Quick answers:

- Upfront credit: Procurement prefers a single upfront invoice that references our PO; we will pay once the PO matches the invoice. If Redwood needs staged invoices we can accommodate, but procurement wants the contract to call out the invoice referencing PO # and an acceptance window (we will confirm exact PO format).

- VAT: Acme will self-account for VAT under local reverse-charge rules. We will provide a tax residency / VAT registration certificate — I’ll attach the certificate to procurement in a follow-up.

I copied our legal contact J. Peterson and procurement. J, procurement: please confirm whether a single upfront invoice (with PO reference) is acceptable for Redwood and whether you need specific wording to confirm reverse-charge.

— Asha
Asha Malik
Director, Strategic Procurement
Acme Corp


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, procurement@acme.com
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 13:15:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Perfect — thanks for the confirmation, Asha. Attaching a redline (Acme_Orderform_redlines_20271025.docx) that:

- Specifies a single upfront invoice for the \"Activation Credit\" referencing PO # (we left a bracket for the exact PO to be inserted by Acme).
- Adds a clause that Redwood will issue the invoice net of VAT if Acme provides valid tax residency / VAT registration evidence; otherwise, Redwood reserves the right to charge VAT in accordance with applicable law.
- Adds an operational sentence: \"Customer shall submit tax documentation within 14 days of execution; failure to provide documentation may result in taxation of invoices and no refund for taxes subsequently reclaimed.\"

Kimberly / Karthik — could you please review the suggested clause below (pulled into the body for quick review) and advise any edits. If OK, we’ll ask Acme Legal to confirm and then push to DocuSign.

Suggested Order Form clause (for quick legal review):
\"Tax and Invoicing: Redwood will invoice Customer for amounts due under this Order Form. If Customer provides valid VAT registration or tax residency documentation prior to issuance of the first invoice, Redwood will issue invoices net of VAT and Customer will self-account for VAT pursuant to applicable law. Customer shall deliver such documentation within fourteen (14) days following Order Form execution. Absent timely delivery of documentation, Redwood may charge VAT on invoices and Customer remains responsible for taxes charged. Any retrospective tax relief granted to Customer after payment shall be handled between the parties in good faith and in accordance with applicable tax law; Redwood shall not be obligated to credit taxes already remitted where such remittance was made in reliance on Customer’s failure to provide documentation.\"

Notes on upfront invoice flow for Finance: we recommend Redwood issue a single invoice labeled \"Activation Credit — Invoice 1 of 1\" for the upfront credit, referencing the PO. Payment will be applied to monthly usage until the credit is exhausted. If Acme wants staged invoicing, we can convert the single invoice to N installments — please confirm.

Ben
Senior AE, Redwood Inference
attachments: Acme_Orderform_redlines_20271025.docx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>, procurement@acme.com, J. Peterson - Acme Legal <j.peterson@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 16:42:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Thanks for the draft language, Ben — good clarity. A few proposed edits from Finance/Legal that I recommend we share as a single change set with Acme Legal to avoid iterative redlines:

1) Tighten the timing: change the 14 days to \"within ten (10) days of execution\" to keep procurement on a short cadence.

2) Add an evidence list: require \"VAT registration certificate, VAT number, and confirmation of reverse-charge applicability (if required)\" as acceptable documentation. That makes it clear what Finance needs to validate the net invoice.

3) Upfront invoice labeling: operationally call out \"Application of Credit\" and an invoice line referencing the credit expiration/cutoff (e.g., \"Credit applies to usage through 12 months from invoice date unless otherwise agreed\"). This avoids ambiguity about when credits lapse.

4) Escrow / withholding contingency: add a fallback sentence that if a tax authority later asserts withholding against Redwood due to incorrect customer documentation, parties will cooperate, but Redwood may be entitled to reimbursement of taxes, penalties, and reasonable costs unless Customer can demonstrate clear error.

Suggested refined clause (one-paragraph, copyable):
\"Tax and Invoicing: Redwood will invoice Customer for amounts due under this Order Form. If Customer provides valid VAT registration documentation (including VAT registration certificate and VAT number) and evidence of the applicability of reverse-charge treatment prior to issuance of the first invoice, Redwood will issue invoices net of VAT and Customer will self-account for VAT pursuant to applicable law. Customer shall deliver such documentation within ten (10) days following Order Form execution. Absent timely delivery of the required documentation, Redwood may charge VAT on invoices and Customer remains responsible for taxes charged. Upfront credits will be invoiced as a single line item labeled \"Activation Credit — Application of Credit\" and will apply to usage for up to twelve (12) months from the invoice date unless otherwise agreed in writing. If, following issuance of invoices net of VAT, a competent tax authority asserts that VAT or withholding is payable by Redwood due to Customer’s documentation being invalid or inapplicable, Customer shall promptly reimburse Redwood for any taxes, penalties, and reasonable costs incurred as a result of such assertion, except where Customer demonstrates that Redwood acted in bad faith.\"

Ben/Karthik — if you both sign off, I’ll send this consolidated language to J. Peterson and ask Acme Procurement to confirm PO formatting. Also recommending we attach the tax evidence link (drive folder above) to the DocuSign packet so Legal can verify before countersignature.

Kimberly Park
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>
Cc: procurement@acme.com
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 09:08:00 +0100
Subject: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Hi Asha,

Thanks again for the productive call this morning. Per our discussion, I attached the current Order Form (Acme_Orderform_v3.pdf). Two items Finance flagged we should lock down before legal redlines:

1) Advance collection (upfront credit) — Acme asked whether the upfront payment can be treated as a prepayment or whether Redwood should invoice and collect in advance and then apply to monthly usage. We usually issue an invoice for the upfront credit and apply it to usage; Finance wants to confirm whether Acme needs any special PO/invoice sequencing.

2) VAT / tax liability — you mentioned that Acme will be responsible for VAT in your jurisdiction, but your procurement team asked whether Redwood can show VAT on our invoice or whether Acme will self-account. If Acme will self-account, we need a tax residency certificate or OSS evidence so we can issue a net invoice.

Can you confirm: (a) preferred invoice flow for the upfront credit (single upfront invoice vs. staged invoices tied to tranches), and (b) whether Acme will provide the tax evidence (attached form noted as Acme_Tax_Cert_2027.pdf) or expects Redwood to tax the invoices?

If you prefer, I can loop in Kimberly from Finance and Karthik from Legal to propose straightforward language for the Order Form to avoid back-and-forth redlines.

Thanks — aiming to get this locked so Legal can finish the MSA/order form review tomorrow.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
attachments: Acme_Orderform_v3.pdf


From: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@acme.com, J. Peterson - Acme Legal <j.peterson@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 10:32:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Ben — thanks, that matches our notes. Quick answers:

- Upfront credit: Procurement prefers a single upfront invoice that references our PO; we will pay once the PO matches the invoice. If Redwood needs staged invoices we can accommodate, but procurement wants the contract to call out the invoice referencing PO # and an acceptance window (we will confirm exact PO format).

- VAT: Acme will self-account for VAT under local reverse-charge rules. We will provide a tax residency / VAT registration certificate — I’ll attach the certificate to procurement in a follow-up.

I copied our legal contact J. Peterson and procurement. J, procurement: please confirm whether a single upfront invoice (with PO reference) is acceptable for Redwood and whether you need specific wording to confirm reverse-charge.

— Asha
Asha Malik
Director, Strategic Procurement
Acme Corp


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, procurement@acme.com
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 13:15:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Perfect — thanks for the confirmation, Asha. Attaching a redline (Acme_Orderform_redlines_20271025.docx) that:

- Specifies a single upfront invoice for the \"Activation Credit\" referencing PO # (we left a bracket for the exact PO to be inserted by Acme).
- Adds a clause that Redwood will issue the invoice net of VAT if Acme provides valid tax residency / VAT registration evidence; otherwise, Redwood reserves the right to charge VAT in accordance with applicable law.
- Adds an operational sentence: \"Customer shall submit tax documentation within 14 days of execution; failure to provide documentation may result in taxation of invoices and no refund for taxes subsequently reclaimed.\"

Kimberly / Karthik — could you please review the suggested clause below (pulled into the body for quick review) and advise any edits. If OK, we’ll ask Acme Legal to confirm and then push to DocuSign.

Suggested Order Form clause (for quick legal review):
\"Tax and Invoicing: Redwood will invoice Customer for amounts due under this Order Form. If Customer provides valid VAT registration or tax residency documentation prior to issuance of the first invoice, Redwood will issue invoices net of VAT and Customer will self-account for VAT pursuant to applicable law. Customer shall deliver such documentation within fourteen (14) days following Order Form execution. Absent timely delivery of documentation, Redwood may charge VAT on invoices and Customer remains responsible for taxes charged. Any retrospective tax relief granted to Customer after payment shall be handled between the parties in good faith and in accordance with applicable tax law; Redwood shall not be obligated to credit taxes already remitted where such remittance was made in reliance on Customer’s failure to provide documentation.\"

Notes on upfront invoice flow for Finance: we recommend Redwood issue a single invoice labeled \"Activation Credit — Invoice 1 of 1\" for the upfront credit, referencing the PO. Payment will be applied to monthly usage until the credit is exhausted. If Acme wants staged invoicing, we can convert the single invoice to N installments — please confirm.

Ben
Senior AE, Redwood Inference
attachments: Acme_Orderform_redlines_20271025.docx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Asha Malik <asha.malik@acme.com>, procurement@acme.com, J. Peterson - Acme Legal <j.peterson@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Oct 2027 16:42:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Order form — advance collection and VAT responsibility

Thanks for the draft language, Ben — good clarity. A few proposed edits from Finance/Legal that I recommend we share as a single change set with Acme Legal to avoid iterative redlines:

1) Tighten the timing: change the 14 days to \"within ten (10) days of execution\" to keep procurement on a short cadence.

2) Add an evidence list: require \"VAT registration certificate, VAT number, and confirmation of reverse-charge applicability (if required)\" as acceptable documentation. That makes it clear what Finance needs to validate the net invoice.

3) Upfront invoice labeling: operationally call out \"Application of Credit\" and an invoice line referencing the credit expiration/cutoff (e.g., \"Credit applies to usage through 12 months from invoice date unless otherwise agreed\"). This avoids ambiguity about when credits lapse.

4) Escrow / withholding contingency: add a fallback sentence that if a tax authority later asserts withholding against Redwood due to incorrect customer documentation, parties will cooperate, but Redwood may be entitled to reimbursement of taxes, penalties, and reasonable costs unless Customer can demonstrate clear error.

Suggested refined clause (one-paragraph, copyable):
\"Tax and Invoicing: Redwood will invoice Customer for amounts due under this Order Form. If Customer provides valid VAT registration documentation (including VAT registration certificate and VAT number) and evidence of the applicability of reverse-charge treatment prior to issuance of the first invoice, Redwood will issue invoices net of VAT and Customer will self-account for VAT pursuant to applicable law. Customer shall deliver such documentation within ten (10) days following Order Form execution. Absent timely delivery of the required documentation, Redwood may charge VAT on invoices and Customer remains responsible for taxes charged. Upfront credits will be invoiced as a single line item labeled \"Activation Credit — Application of Credit\" and will apply to usage for up to twelve (12) months from the invoice date unless otherwise agreed in writing. If, following issuance of invoices net of VAT, a competent tax authority asserts that VAT or withholding is payable by Redwood due to Customer’s documentation being invalid or inapplicable, Customer shall promptly reimburse Redwood for any taxes, penalties, and reasonable costs incurred as a result of such assertion, except where Customer demonstrates that Redwood acted in bad faith.\"

Ben/Karthik — if you both sign off, I’ll send this consolidated language to J. Peterson and ask Acme Procurement to confirm PO formatting. Also recommending we attach the tax evidence link (drive folder above) to the DocuSign packet so Legal can verify before countersignature.

Kimberly Park
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

title: Third-party roster reconciliation + transfer route verification for Fintegrate
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Luca Rossi <luca@fintegrate.io>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Third-party roster reconciliation + transfer route verification for Fintegrate

Hi Luca,

Quick follow-up from our onboarding call — Fintegrate is listed as a downstream processor in the DPA we sent, and Legal has asked for a short reconciliation of your current third-party roster and the specific transfer routes (e.g., EU->US via subprocessor X, EU->EEA via partner Y) that you rely on for data necessary to serve our integration.

Could you:
- Share the most recent vendor roster (CSV) with columns for vendor name, function, country of processing, and whether they have access to customer data or only metadata.
- Confirm any persistent cross-border transfers from the EU for logs/backups/analytics and the legal basis (SCCs/EEA contracts/other).
- If there are planned vendor changes in the next 90 days, flag them and indicate whether they touch customer content.

If it helps, I attached the template we use to intake vendor rosters. Aim for the roster and a short note by COB PT Monday so Legal can clear the path for our SOC review.

Thanks —

Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales (Platform)
Redwood Inference
[attachment: vendor_roster_template.csv (CSV, 12KB)]

From: Luca Rossi <luca@fintegrate.io>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: compliance@fintegrate.io
Date: Fri, 22 Oct 2027 12:05:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Third-party roster reconciliation + transfer route verification for Fintegrate

Avery —

Apologies for the delay; thanks for the clear checklist. Attached are: vendor_roster_v2.csv (our current inventory), a high-level dataflow diagram, and our SCC submission packet. Highlights below:

- Vendor roster (see vendor_roster_v2.csv): we mark vendors with access to customer content in the "access_level" column.
- EU transfers: primary cross-border transfer is to our analytics provider (NorthMetrics) for aggregated usage metrics — they only receive pseudonymized metrics. Backups are stored in Azure West US; backups contain encrypted blobs (customer keys managed by KMS).
- Legal basis: SCCs in place with NorthMetrics and Azure; DocuSign link to executed SCCs included.
- Planned changes: migrating part of telemetry processing to EdgeSight (Q1 2028). EdgeSight will only handle aggregated telemetry and will not receive content-level data.

Let me know if you need raw samples or a short attestation from our DPO.

Cheers,

Luca Rossi
Head of Integrations
Fintegrate

[attachment: dataflow-diagram.png (PNG, 240KB)]
[attachment: Fintegrate_SCC_submission.pdf (PDF, 320KB)]

Hi Luca — thanks for the materials, and for calling out EdgeSight. A couple of narrow asks to close the ticket from our side:

title: Alignment: buying-playbook, legal routing, and SLO targets from today's discovery
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medlytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Oct 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Alignment: buying-playbook, legal routing, and SLO targets from today's discovery

Hi Sanjay —

Thanks again for the thoughtful call earlier. Quick recap and suggested next steps so we stay aligned with Medlytics' internal buying playbook: 

Summary (from our notes):
- Primary use case: real-time clinical triage assistant for remote nursing triage (short prompt, iterative clarifications).
- Traffic shape: expected pilot ~50 QPS peak, steady-state target 200 QPS across US evening peaks; mostly short context windows (~1k tokens).
- Must-haves: P95 tail latency target, formal procurement steps, and SOC2 / evidence for logging and retention.

Clarifying asks for your procurement loop (can you confirm the items below?):
1) Economic buyer: you mentioned Priya Shah (CPO). Can you confirm she should be looped for pricing / commercial terms?
2) Legal / procurement: who owns contract redlines — Marta in procurement or an outside counsel? Any PO / SSO requirements?
3) Security evidence: initial ask was SOC2 report + data retention policy + architecture diagram for VPC/private deployment. Do you prefer a hosted integration first or a private VPC install for the pilot?
4) SLOs and decision criteria: you stated latency is critical — can you confirm the p95 target we should design for? (We captured 200ms p95 for 1k token interactive queries on the call; please correct if off.) Also helpful to confirm a target unit-cost threshold (e.g., $ per 1M tokens or $/concurrent-connection) for procurement scoring.

Suggested next steps:
- If Priya and Marta are the right stakeholders, could we schedule a 30-min sync this week (exec intro + procurement path)?
- We'll prepare a short procurement packet: SOC2 snapshot, architecture diagram for Hosted / Dedicated / VPC options, and a recommended SLO playbook for pilot.
- Internal action: Karthik and I will draft a recommended configuration that meets a 200ms p95 target (if confirmed) and include caching/batching levers to reduce cost.

Attachments: I attached a lightweight procurement checklist and a one-page pilot MOP draft for your review.

Thanks — happy to adjust based on Priya/Marta preferences.

Ben Carter
Account Exec | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

[attachment: medlytics-mop-draft.pdf (1 page)]
[attachment: redwood-procurement-checklist.xlsx]


From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medlytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@medlytics.com>, Marta Lopez <marta.procurement@medlytics.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Oct 2027 09:06:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Alignment: buying-playbook, legal routing, and SLO targets from today's discovery

Ben —

Thanks for the concise recap, that matches our notes. Quick answers inline: 

1) Economic buyer: Confirmed — Priya Shah (CPO) will be the economic approver for pilots > $50k. Loop her on commercial comms.
2) Legal/procurement: Marta owns day-to-day procurement and will coordinate PO/terms; legal redlines go to our counsel (CC'd on later threads if needed). Marta will own the security questionnaire routing.
3) Security evidence: SOC2 Type II is required for our scoring; we also need a short network diagram showing where data is processed (cloud region + VPC controls) and a statement on whether logs include PII and how long they're retained. For the pilot we prefer a VPC/private deployment (Medlytics requires customer-managed networking for PHI adjacency).
4) SLOs and cost: Latency — 200ms p95 for single-turn interactive prompts (typical 600–1k token requests) is our internal bar for clinician experience. Cost — procurement will score options below ~$120 per 1M tokens (we use 1M tokens as a relative benchmark across vendors). If you can provide guidance on expected cost reduction using caching + batching, that's very helpful for our procurement rubric.

Next steps: Please prepare the procurement packet and include a short runbook that shows: (a) expected p95 at pilot QPS (50) and at 200 QPS, (b) recommended deployment (VPC vs Dedicated vs Hosted) with tradeoffs, and (c) procurement checklist filled where possible.

We can do an exec intro call Thursday 10/21 at 11:00am ET — Priya and Marta are available then. 

Sanjay Rao
Head of Product — Clinical Automation
Medlytics Inc.
sanjay@medlytics.com

On Tue, Oct 19, 2027 at 08:45 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks again — we'll get that packet ready.


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medlytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Oct 2027 12:38:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Alignment: buying-playbook, legal routing, and SLO targets from today's discovery

Sanjay / Ben —

Appreciate the clarity. A couple of technical commitments and what we'll include in the packet to make procurement evaluation straightforward: 

What we'll commit in the packet:
- Baseline SLO modeling: predicted p95 latency at 50 QPS (pilot) and at 200 QPS (steady-state) for three deployment options: Hosted (multi-tenant), Dedicated GPU pool, and Private VPC (customer VPC peering). We will include 95th percentile numbers across realistic prompt lengths (300, 600, 1k tokens).
- Cost modeling: estimated $/1M tokens baseline for each option plus delta when enabling KV caching and reduced precision quantization (where model-compatible). We'll show an example that reduces token costs by ~20–40% for cacheable prompts.
- Operational notes: autoscaling warm-up, burst capacity, and recommended fallback routing to a lower-cost variant if SLOs cannot be met during regional outages.
- Security artifacts: SOC2 snapshot, retention policy doc, and a network diagram for the VPC option.

Questions we still need from you (to make numbers precise):
A) Traffic mix: percentage of cacheable vs unique prompts (e.g., templated triage flows vs free text). Even a rough split (60/40) helps cost modeling.
B) Pilot acceptance criteria: aside from the 200ms p95, is there an accuracy or hallucination threshold we should capture (e.g., manual review pass-rate)?

I'll attach the draft procurement checklist and a one-page MOP; Ben will send both to Priya/Marta ahead of Thursday.

—Karthik
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com

[attachment: redwood-procurement-checklist.xlsx]
[attachment: medlytics-mop-draft.pdf]


From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medlytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@medlytics.com>, Marta Lopez <marta.procurement@medlytics.com>
Date: Wed, 20 Oct 2027 09:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Alignment: buying-playbook, legal routing, and SLO targets from today's discovery

Perfect — that level of detail will work. Quick answers:
- Traffic mix: currently estimate ~70% template-driven/structured prompts (cacheable), ~30% free-text follow-ups.
- Pilot acceptance: Clinician satisfaction score (CSAT) > 85% on a 50-case pilot and no P95 breaches > 3% of calls. We'll provide more specifics before the pilot starts.

Confirming exec intro: Thursday 10/21 at 11:00am ET with Priya and Marta. Please send calendar invite and procurement packet by COB Wednesday 10/20.

Sanjay

--
Forwarded for convenience: Priya Shah (CPO) contact: priya.shah@medlytics.com

preview:
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Q4 executive headcount certification — request and ask

Stephanie / Aditya,

Attached is the Q4 exec headcount request package we discussed in the SLT meeting. Summary below:

- Requests: 3 executive-level roles (VP Product Ops, Head of Enterprise Sales, Director of Trust & Safety)
- Total incremental HC: 3 FTEs (impact on run-rate included in the spreadsheet)
- Compensation ask: two roles exceed current band caps by ~8–12% (details in offer_matrix_v2.pdf)

We need:
1) Certification from execs to proceed with offers by next Wednesday (2027-10-20).
2) Delegation approval so recruiting can extend offers up to the indicated exception amounts without additional sign-off.

Can you review and confirm whether you are okay delegating sign authority to me (and recruiting) for the two band exceptions? If not, please indicate who should sign and any conditions.

Attachments: Q4_exec_hc_request.xlsx, offer_matrix_v2.pdf

Thanks,
Marcus
Marcus Lin
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference


From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Ellen Park <ellen.park@talentadvisors.io>
Date: 2027-10-14T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Q4 executive headcount certification — request and ask

Marcus — thanks for the packet. Quick read-through notes below; see inline.

On 2027-10-14 09:12, Marcus Lin wrote:
> - Requests: 3 executive-level roles (VP Product Ops, Head of Enterprise Sales, Director of Trust & Safety)
> - Total incremental HC: 3 FTEs (impact on run-rate included in the spreadsheet)

I support the need for these roles given the forecasted deals and compliance roadmap.

> - Compensation ask: two roles exceed current band caps by ~8–12% (details in offer_matrix_v2.pdf)

I need clearer justification for the band exceptions before delegating sign authority. Please add a short note for each role covering: 1) market comp comps (benchmarks), 2) plan to pressure-test with candidate counteroffers, and 3) any retention/legal clawback language. Ellen — can you drop a 1-paragraph market comp summary into the doc by EOD?

On delegation: I am amenable to delegating to Marcus for offers up to +10% over band for VP Product Ops only, provided:
- Recruiting confirms candidate is mission-critical and has competing offers;
- Offer includes 12-month retention vesting;
- Compensation committee (Aditya + me) will be notified within 24 hours of offer acceptance.

For the Head of Enterprise Sales and Director Trust & Safety I want full exec sign-off (Aditya + me) before any exceptions beyond band.

Please revise and re-share. Also cc legal if there are special terms.

— Stephanie
Stephanie Nguyen
VP, People & Operations
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-14T13:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Q4 executive headcount certification — request and ask

Thanks — read and agree with Stephanie's guardrails. A couple of additions from finance:
- We can approve a one-time exception pool for Q4 equal to 3% of the exec salary budget, to be managed centrally. Any individual offer > +10% must escalate to CFO for one-off approval.
- Please include the modeled runway impact if all three roles hire at midpoint vs. +10%.

Marcus: if you can incorporate the market notes (Ellen) and the finance runway table, I'm comfortable with the delegation as described for VP Product Ops. For the other two roles, Aditya + Stephanie sign-off required.

Aditya
Aditya Rao
President, Redwood Inference


From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Ellen Park <ellen.park@talentadvisors.io>
Date: 2027-10-14T17:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Q4 executive headcount certification — request and ask

1) Delegation: Marcus is authorized to extend an offer for VP Product Ops up to +10% above band with retention vesting and 24-hour notify to Aditya + me. Recruiter to use the offer_matrix_v2.pdf template and include standard clawback language.

2) Head of Enterprise Sales & Director Trust & Safety: offers may be prepared and shared with Aditya + me for sign-off; no exceptions beyond band without CFO escalation.

3) Ellen: please upload the one-paragraph market comp summaries into the Drive folder and update offer_matrix_v2.pdf by tomorrow morning. Marcus / Recruiting: update the spreadsheet with the finance runway scenarios (midpoint vs +10%).

I've saved a signed copy of the delegation note in the Drive folder. Attaching the updated file for recruiting records.

Attachments: delegation_note_signed.pdf

— Stephanie

title: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2027 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay

Team —

Quick ask: VerdantPay (Samir Khan / Leah Ortiz) are asking for a one-off increase to their sandbox credits and an extension of their POC window beyond our standard PLG limits. Key context:

- Current trial: 30-day sandbox + 50k complimentary tokens (PLG default).
- Ask: +60k complimentary tokens (total request 110k) and an additional 45 days on the POC (so a total ~75-day window).
- Why: they completed core integration (payments risk scoring) and ran a small production-like workload; latency and accuracy look good. They need runway to run a broader transaction mix and validate risk thresholds with their compliance team.
- Opportunity: Marissa estimates a ~$1.2M ARR TCV if they convert to a dedicated deployment and commit to a 12-month reserved plan.

I attached a usage snapshot and the latest MAP. The customer is time-sensitive — they want a yes/no by EOD Thursday so they can schedule an internal compliance review.

title: SOC 2 re-attestation, log retention window and seat increment for renewal
preview:
From: Lisa Park <lisa.park@acmecloud.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, procurement@acmecloud.com
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: SOC 2 re-attestation + retention window for renewal

Hi Ava,

We're starting our renewal packet for Acme Cloud (renewal target 2027-12-01) and have a couple of asks that will affect our procurement timeline and commit size:

1) SOC 2 re-attestation: Our security team needs the latest SOC 2 report plus confirmation of any scope changes since the last attestation (we saw a mention of new telemetry exports last quarter).
2) Audit log retention: We need a guaranteed 13-month retention window for raw audit logs (forensics team requirement). Current contract states 90 days. Please confirm whether you can extend retention or provide a managed export cadence.
3) Capacity/seat increase: We plan to expand from 200 to 400 seats if the above is satisfied; procurement wants a draft amendment (pricing and timeline).

Can you share a plan, expected dates for evidence transfer, and a single point of contact for security and procurement? We will need any evidence transfer to be completed by mid-November to make the December renewal target.

Attached: PO-Acme-1127.pdf (procurement placeholder)

Thanks,
Lisa
Lisa Park
Security Program Manager
Acme Cloud
---


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Park <lisa.park@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2027 16:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SOC 2 re-attestation + retention window for renewal

Hi Lisa — thanks for the note and the PO stub.

We're happy to support the renewal and the expansion. High-level plan below, and I've attached a one-page MAP and an evidence upload guide for your security team.

Proposed timeline (short):
- By 2027-10-18: Redwood provides latest SOC 2 attestation PDF and scope addendum; deliver via secure link.
- By 2027-10-25: Agree on log retention option: (A) extend managed retention to 13 months (Redwood-managed, additional storage charge), or (B) weekly managed export to Acme S3.
- By 2027-11-08: Evidence upload complete (our secure uplink) so your auditors can review.
- By 2027-11-20: Amendment signed / seat commit finalized (DocuSign link to follow).

Notes:
- For immediate access to SOC artifacts, see attached SOC2-2023-Attestation.pdf (redacted). Full evidence package will be through the secure upload process in the attached instructions.
- For retention, option (B) is the fastest short-term path; we can provision export jobs on a weekly cadence and maintain a rolling 13-month export set in your S3. Long-term managed retention can be added to the contract (pricing in the MAP).

Next steps I suggest: 1) Security team confirms preferred retention option, 2) procurement confirms who will sign the amendment and timing, 3) we schedule a 30-minute sync (security + procurement + Redwood sales/ops).

Attachments: MAP-Acme-2027.xlsx, evidence-upload-instructions.docx, SOC2-2023-Attestation.pdf

Best,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
(415) 555-0188

- Proposed: 400 seats @ 10% discount on list with a 12-month annual commit (savings vs month-to-month).

Thanks Ava and Ben — helpful. A few clarifications from security/procurement: 

- Retention: Our preference is Redwood-managed 13-month retention (Option A) if the incremental cost is reasonable. Could you provide an estimated monthly storage bucket size based on our current telemetry? Last quarter we averaged ~3.4 TB of raw audit logs per month.
- Evidence: Security requests the full attestation PDF and a manifest of the control evidence you will provide (names of artifacts and expected delivery dates).
- Timeline: The internal deadline to route the amendment to legal is 2027-11-22, so the evidence upload complete by 2027-11-08 is critical.

Lisa

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Park <lisa.park@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 Oct 2027 11:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SOC 2 re-attestation + retention window for renewal

Hi Lisa — appreciate the specifics. Confirming next steps and answers below; updated MAP attached and I'll open the DocuSign amendment once you confirm retention option.

1) Storage estimate: Based on 3.4 TB/month raw logs, 13-month Redwood-managed retention ~= 44.2 TB total. Monthly incremental storage charge estimate: 44.2 TB * 1024 GB/TB * $0.02/GB/month = approx $905/month (we will provide a line-item with exact GB rounding in the MAP). One-time migration/setup: $5,000.

2) Evidence manifest: see attached MAP-Acme-2027.xlsx (tab: EvidenceManifest) — lists artifacts (SOC2 PDF, control test artifacts, access logs, change logs) and target delivery dates.
